original_hypothesis.title: TVL calculation can be bricked by a single reverting position, halting deposits/withdrawals and fee workflows
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: TVLHelper.getTVL iterates all holding positions and calls connector.getPositionTVL; any revert bubbles up and reverts TVL(), which is used in previewDeposit/previewRedeem and thus in calculateDepositShares/calculateWithdrawShares/recordProfitForFee/deposit limit checks. A single position tied to a protocol outage, misconfigured oracle route, or a connector reverting for malformed state can halt core operations.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is an operationally common failure mode in production: external protocol changes, paused pools, or oracle feed issues can cause view calls to revert. Because TVL() is upstream of state-changing flows, this becomes a system-wide liveness risk and an attacker can potentially induce it by creating/keeping a problematic position (if they can influence connector state via manager escalation).

proof_of_signal.pattern: for each position: IConnector(...).getPositionTVL(...); no try/catch; revert propagates

preconditions: At least one holding position exists that can revert in getPositionTVL (oracle revert, external protocol revert, bad additionalData decode, etc.)
TVL() is called in critical paths (deposit limit checks, ERC4626 previews)
No fallback mode exists for partial TVL

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: TVL is a hard dependency for queue calculations and deposit gating.
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Not necessarily; an external protocol outage can trigger
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Any time, especially during external protocol incidents

operational_questions: Which connectors can revert in _getPositionTVL due to external protocol behavior?
What is the runbook for removing/ignoring a broken position if Registry.onlyVaultMaintainer is itself broken?
Is there an emergency TVL override or ability to pause deposits/withdraws safely?
validation_requirements: Fuzz test TVL with mocked connector that reverts; observe effect on calculateDepositShares/calculateWithdrawShares
Review each connector's _getPositionTVL external calls for revert conditions
Assess operational ability to remove the position from holdingPositions under current registry access controls

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Create or keep a holding position that will revert on valuation (e.g., oracle route missing causing NoyaOracle_PriceOracleUnavailable)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Ensure position remains in registry holdingPositions
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Any user/manager action relying on TVL reverts, halting deposits/withdraws
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: May require manager to create the position; can also occur naturally due to external changes

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: High: bricked deposit/withdraw processing and fee bookkeeping
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: High if registry admin functions are inaccessible (see onlyVaultMaintainer bug)

execution_requirements_analysis.operational_timing: Most likely during outages or misconfiguration events

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Reverts in TVL() call traces; oracle unavailable errors
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Repeated failed keeper runs due to valuation revert

validation_result.mechanism: Missing try/catch in TVL iteration causes system-wide DoS
validation_result.impact_summary: A single reverting position in any connector's getPositionTVL bricks all deposit, withdrawal, and fee operations because TVLHelper.getTVL has no error handling around external calls.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: TVLHelper.getTVL (lines 10-33) iterates all holding positions and directly calls IConnector(positions[i].calculatorConnector).getPositionTVL(positions[i], baseToken) without any try/catch wrapper. If any single connector's getPositionTVL reverts (due to oracle unavailability, stale Chainlink data, external protocol pausing, malformed additionalData decoding, etc.), the entire getTVL function reverts. Since TVL() in AccountingManager depends on getTVL, and TVL() is called by totalAssets() which feeds into ERC4626's previewDeposit/previewRedeem, this cascades into failures in: deposit() (TVL limit check), calculateDepositShares() (previewDeposit call), calculateWithdrawShares() (previewRedeem call), recordProfitForFee() (getProfit -> TVL call), and checkIfTVLHasDroped(). This constitutes a system-wide denial of service triggered by any single problematic position.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The code evidence directly supports the hypothesis. TVLHelper.sol lines 17-22 show the unprotected loop making external calls. AccountingManager.sol shows TVL() being called in deposit (line with TVL() > depositLimitTotalAmount), calculateDepositShares (via previewDeposit), calculateWithdrawShares (via previewRedeem), and recordProfitForFee (via getProfit). Multiple connector implementations make external calls that can revert: ChainlinkOracleConnector reverts on stale data (NoyaChainlinkOracle_DATA_OUT_OF_DATE), NoyaValueOracle reverts when no oracle route exists (NoyaOracle_PriceOracleUnavailable), and protocol-specific connectors call external contracts that may be paused or misconfigured.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: No try/catch around the external call IConnector(...).getPositionTVL(...). Any revert from a single position propagates up and reverts the entire TVL calculation.
TVL() directly depends on getTVL which can revert. TVL() is called by totalAssets() -> previewDeposit/previewRedeem, deposit() limit check, getProfit() -> recordProfitForFee, and checkIfTVLHasDroped.
deposit() calls TVL() for limit checking. If TVL() reverts due to a problematic position, no new deposits can be made.
previewDeposit calls totalAssets() -> TVL(). If TVL reverts, calculateDepositShares cannot process any queued deposits.
previewRedeem calls totalAssets() -> TVL(). If TVL reverts, calculateWithdrawShares cannot process any queued withdrawals.

execution_path_analysis.entry_point: deposit(address,uint256,address) or calculateDepositShares(uint256) or calculateWithdrawShares(uint256) or recordProfitForFee()
calling_sequence: User/Manager calls deposit() / calculateDepositShares() / calculateWithdrawShares() / recordProfitForFee()
These call TVL() -> TVLHelper.getTVL(vaultId, registry, baseToken) or totalAssets() -> TVL()
If ANY connector's getPositionTVL reverts (oracle down, stale price, external protocol paused, etc.)
Revert propagates up through getTVL -> TVL() -> totalAssets() -> previewDeposit/previewRedeem -> calling function
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: TVLHelper.getTVL (line 19): `uint256 tvl = IConnector(positions[i].calculatorConnector).getPositionTVL(positions[i], baseToken);` — This is an unprotected external call inside a loop. The revert propagation path is: getPositionTVL reverts -> getTVL reverts -> TVL() reverts -> totalAssets() reverts -> previewDeposit/previewRedeem reverts -> calculateDepositShares/calculateWithdrawShares/deposit/recordProfitForFee reverts. Specific revert sources include: ChainlinkOracleConnector.getValueFromChainlinkFeed can revert with NoyaChainlinkOracle_DATA_OUT_OF_DATE if block.timestamp - updatedAt > chainlinkPriceAgeThreshold; NoyaValueOracle._getValue can revert with NoyaOracle_PriceOracleUnavailable if no oracle route is configured; any connector's _getPositionTVL that calls abi.decode on additionalData can revert on malformed data; any connector calling external protocol functions can revert if the protocol is paused.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: No deployment scripts provided that would contradict the hypothesis. Deployment scripts show standard connector and AccountingManager deployment with no special error-handling configuration.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: The scenario is realistic and can occur naturally without any adversarial action. Common triggers include: (1) Chainlink oracle feed going stale for >2 hours (the configured threshold), causing ChainlinkOracleConnector to revert. (2) An external protocol (Curve, Balancer, Pendle, etc.) being paused, causing its view functions to revert. (3) A maintainer misconfiguring oracle routes, causing NoyaValueOracle to revert with PriceOracleUnavailable. (4) A connector's additionalData being malformed after a position update, causing abi.decode to revert. An adversarial scenario could involve a compromised or malicious manager creating a position whose oracle route is then removed by the maintainer, permanently bricking TVL until the position is removed. Recovery is complicated by the Registry.onlyVaultMaintainer bug (AND instead of OR logic) which may prevent the maintainer from removing the problematic connector.
validation_result.impact_assessment: HIGH severity. When triggered, this bricks all core vault operations: no new deposits can be processed (deposit() reverts on TVL limit check), no deposit share calculations can occur (calculateDepositShares reverts on previewDeposit), no withdrawal share calculations can occur (calculateWithdrawShares reverts on previewRedeem), no performance fee recording can occur (recordProfitForFee reverts on getProfit), and no TVL drop checking can occur (checkIfTVLHasDroped reverts on getProfit). User funds are not directly stolen but are locked — users cannot deposit or withdraw. The emergency pause mechanism (emergencyStop) still works since it doesn't depend on TVL, but unpause doesn't resolve the underlying issue.
recommended_mitigations: Wrap the IConnector.getPositionTVL call in TVLHelper.getTVL with a try/catch block. On revert, either skip the position (treating its TVL as 0) or emit an event and continue. Example: try IConnector(positions[i].calculatorConnector).getPositionTVL(positions[i], baseToken) returns (uint256 tvl) { ... } catch { emit PositionTVLFailed(positions[i].calculatorConnector, positions[i].positionId); continue; }
Add an emergency function callable by the emergency role to forcibly remove a holding position from the registry, bypassing the normal connector-based removal flow.
Fix the Registry.onlyVaultMaintainer modifier (AND vs OR bug) so the maintainer can actually remove problematic connectors/positions without also needing the EMERGENCY_ROLE.
Consider implementing a TVL cache with a staleness window: if the live TVL calculation reverts, fall back to the last known good TVL value within a configurable time window.
Add per-connector circuit breakers: if a connector's TVL calculation has reverted recently, allow skipping it in TVL calculations until manually re-enabled.
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy a mock connector whose getPositionTVL always reverts. 2. Register it as an enabled connector and add a holding position pointing to it. 3. Call AccountingManager.TVL() and observe it reverts. 4. Call deposit() with valid amount and observe it reverts. 5. Queue a deposit, then call calculateDepositShares() and observe it reverts. 6. Queue a withdrawal, then call calculateWithdrawShares() and observe it reverts. 7. Call recordProfitForFee() and observe it reverts. 8. Verify that removing the mock connector's position restores normal operation.
uncertainties: The exact recovery time depends on whether the Registry.onlyVaultMaintainer bug (AND vs OR) actually prevents the maintainer from removing the problematic position. If the maintainer also holds EMERGENCY_ROLE, recovery is faster.
Some external protocol reverts may be temporary (e.g., Chainlink oracle updates after a brief outage), so the DoS window may self-resolve in some cases.
additional_testing_needed: Test with each concrete connector implementation (Curve, Pendle, Balancer, Morpho, etc.) to catalog specific revert conditions in their _getPositionTVL functions.
Test the Registry.onlyVaultMaintainer modifier to confirm the AND vs OR bug and assess its impact on emergency recovery for this DoS scenario.
Fuzz test TVLHelper.getTVL with various combinations of reverting and non-reverting positions to confirm the all-or-nothing behavior.
Test recovery procedures: can the emergency role use Registry.changeVaultAddresses to work around the maintainer modifier bug?
